<div class="blog-header">

## [A Motorcycle for the Mind](https://nav.al/ai)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

On AI and the Future of Work

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

Hey, this is Nivi. You’re listening to the Naval Podcast. For the first
time in recorded history, we are not at the same location. I am actually
walking around town and Naval might be doing the same, so there might be
some ambient noise, but we are going to try hard to remove that with AI
and some good audio
engineering.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/ai" class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Curate People](https://nav.al/curate-people)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

On recruiting and culture

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

You’re listening to the Naval Podcast. Today we’re going to be talking
about recruiting, hiring, team, and culture. There’s a famous quote from
[Vinod Khosla, “The team you build is the company you
build.”](https://x.com/vkhosla/status/1445973802282745857?lang=en) Or in
other words, they told you it was a technology game when it’s really a
recruiting game.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/curate-people" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Apr 27 2012

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Build a Team that Ships](https://nav.al/build-a-team-that-ships)

</div>

<div class="content">

I started my first company 15 years go, and I still can’t manage. I
suspect that very few people can. With AngelList, we want a team of
self-managing people who ship code. Here’s what we do: Keep the team
small. All doers, no talkers. Absolutely no middle managers. All BD via
APIs.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/build-a-team-that-ships"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Apr 27 2012

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Dec 13 2011

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Why You Can’t Hire](https://nav.al/why-you-cant-hire)

</div>

<div class="content">

There isn’t a shortage of developers and designers. There’s a surplus of
founders. The cost of starting a company has collapsed. It’s now just
(minimal) salaries. For entrepreneurs, desks are free, hosting is free,
marketing is online, and company setup is cheap. Raising the first \$25K
for product development is easy – join an
incubator.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/why-you-cant-hire" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Dec 13 2011

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

May 14 2010

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Interview on Entrepreneurship up at GigaOm TV](https://nav.al/interview-on-entrepreneurship-up-at-gigaom-tv)

</div>

<div class="content">

[Check out the full article, and watch the
video.](http://gigaom.com/2010/05/13/video-want-to-be-an-entrepreneur-listen-to-naval-ravikant/)<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>

class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

May 14 2010

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Mar 5 2010

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Docverse and Mixer Labs exit stage right](https://nav.al/docverse-and-mixer-labs-exit-stage-right)

</div>

<div class="content">

Congratulations to portfolio companies
[DocVerse](http://techcrunch.com/2010/03/05/google-acquires-docverse-to-further-office-arms-race/)
(now at Google) and [Mixer
Labs](http://blog.twitter.com/2009/12/mixing-it-up-at-795-folsom-st.html)
(now at Twitter). The best part was getting to know and work with people
that I genuinely liked and now consider
friends.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/docverse-and-mixer-labs-exit-stage-right"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Mar 5 2010

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Feb 8 2010

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Y Combinator vs. Graduate School](https://nav.al/y-combinator-vs-graduate-school)

</div>

<div class="content">

Y Combinator\* is the new Graduate School. In some ways, it’s better: –
You pay to go to graduate school. YC pays you. – After school, you get a
job. After YC, you create jobs. – You repeat the works of the greats in
school. YC expects you to do original
work.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/y-combinator-vs-graduate-school"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Feb 8 2010

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Jan 17 2010

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Why You Need to be in Silicon Valley](https://nav.al/why-you-need-to-be-in-silicon-valley)

</div>

<div class="content">

For years I didn’t believe this. I thought that you could take advantage
of the benefits of Boston, Seattle, NY, Austin – cheaper talent, no echo
chamber, local Universities, etc.. But I give up. I found myself telling
an entrepreneur why he had to be in Silicon Valley if he wanted to
succeed.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/why-you-need-to-be-in-silicon-valley"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Jan 17 2010

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Nov 12 2009

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [How to Pick a Co-Founder (at Venture Hacks)](https://nav.al/how-to-pick-a-co-founder-at-venture-hacks)

</div>

<div class="content">

I have a new post on picking a co-founder, up here:
<http://venturehacks.com/articles/pick-cofounder><span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/how-to-pick-a-co-founder-at-venture-hacks"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Nov 12 2009

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Nov 9 2009

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [The returns to entrepreneurship](https://nav.al/the-returns-to-entrepreneurship)

</div>

<div class="content">

I was at dinner the other night with a group of entrepreneurs. One told
the story of a 27-year-old whiz kid whose company will likely exit for
\$500M – \$1B – the business now being less than two years old. You can
imagine the effect that this had on the brilliant, hardworking 35+
entrepreneurs in the group, who have had their share of hits, but not at
that magnitude and not that
quickly.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/the-returns-to-entrepreneurship"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Nov 9 2009

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>
